transfer_conclusions.xd: XD-Violence shows strong binary transfer with AUC/AP=0.835726 / 0.884683 and transfer ratio XD/UCF=0.901168. Overlap-class transfer is partial (macro-F1=0.360842), with stronger classes like explosion and weaker classes like abuse, consistent with uneven class semantics across datasets.
transfer_conclusions.rwf: RWF-2000 fight validation shows weak explicit fight transfer at precision/recall/F1=1.000000 / 0.140000 / 0.245614, while auxiliary anomaly ranking remains useful (AUC/AP=0.862250 / 0.818835). This pattern supports conservative decoding behavior as a key bottleneck rather than total feature collapse.
transfer_conclusions.shanghaitech: ShanghaiTech robustness is negative with AUC/AP=0.370528 / 0.216516 and severe false-negative dominance (FP=0, FN=43). This indicates strong out-of-domain shift where a violence-trained representation does not generalize to broader crowd-centric anomaly patterns.

integrated_interpretability_conclusion: Step 14A shows external errors dominated by similar normal motion(267), low light(155), crowd density(43), other(29). Step 14B indicates diffuse/high-entropy attention on failure cases, with weak focus on actionable segments under domain shift. Step 14C shows partial XD/RWF semantic alignment but clear ShanghaiTech out-of-domain drift; false negatives are relatively more normal-adjacent than true positives. Step 14D finds boundary refinement currently weak/noisy (mean tIoU delta=-0.032093) with weak boundary-confidence alignment (corr=0.021262).
failure_taxonomy_summary: External transfer failures are primarily associated with low light, similar normal motion, crowd density/domain shift, and conservative positive prediction behavior that drives false-negative-heavy outcomes.

supported: XD zero-shot binary transfer supported (ratio XD/UCF=0.901168, pass_75=True).
partially_supported: RWF partially supported: ranking signal transfers (AUC=0.862250) but fight recall/F1 remain weak (recall=0.140000, F1=0.245614).
not_supported: Boundary refinement benefit not supported (mean tIoU delta=-0.032093).

overall_project_verdict.summary: Project succeeded overall on core objectives: strong in-domain performance and meaningful zero-shot XD binary transfer, with partial support for fight-specific transfer on RWF and non-support for ShanghaiTech robustness. Interpretability goals are supported and provide a coherent explanation of where transfer fails and why.

report_ready_abstract: We developed and evaluated a weakly supervised violence-detection system based on RTFM with classifier, TRN temporal modeling, and boundary-aware decoding under a fixed UCF-trained protocol. On UCF-Crime, the final model maintained strong in-domain detection and localization behavior relative to earlier baselines. In zero-shot XD-Violence transfer, binary anomaly ranking remained strong, while overlap-class performance showed uneven semantic transfer across categories. RWF-2000 fight validation showed useful anomaly ranking but weak fight-label recall, indicating conservative decision behavior in external transfer. ShanghaiTech robustness was negative, with false-negative-dominant behavior and poor out-of-domain generalization. Interpretability analyses linked these outcomes to diffuse attention on failure cases, partial cross-dataset feature alignment, and weak boundary-confidence utility. Overall, the system supports strong in-domain and limited cross-dataset transfer claims, while highlighting domain-shift and conservative decoding as primary failure drivers.

step14b_takeaways.ucf_attention: UCF attention is more diffuse on selected cases (mean entropy=0.999).
step14b_takeaways.xd_attention: XD errors are weak-focus dominant on selected cases (mean pred-overlap=0.000, failure_frac=0.33).
step14b_takeaways.rwf_attention: RWF failures show under-attention to fight motion (failure_frac=0.50, mean pred-overlap=0.000).
step14b_takeaways.sh_attention: ShanghaiTech collapse appears as domain mismatch in attention (failure_frac=0.67, mean entropy=0.997).

step14c_report_sentence: t-SNE shows external-set false negatives clustering closer to the normal manifold with diffuse feature organization, while cross-dataset positive semantics (especially RWF/ShanghaiTech) drift from UCF violent structure.
